intervalProtocols.name: Zone 2 Low-Intensity
intervalProtocols.subtitle: Aerobic base builder
intervalProtocols.notes: Should be able to speak in full sentences throughout. Try to breathe only through nose — if you cannot, slow down.

intervalProtocols.name: Norwegian 2×4 Intervals
intervalProtocols.subtitle: Phase 1 adaptation
intervalProtocols.notes: Introduce from Week 5. Zone 4 = hard but not maximal — you can speak single words only.

intervalProtocols.name: Norwegian 3×4 Intervals
intervalProtocols.subtitle: Phase 2 progression
intervalProtocols.notes: May require shortening strength block to fit total session time.

intervalProtocols.name: Full Norwegian 4×4
intervalProtocols.subtitle: Phase 3 — Wisløff protocol
intervalProtocols.notes: Combine with shortened strength circuit (15 min strength + 30 min 4×4).

breathingExercises.name: Diaphragmatic Breathing Reset
breathingExercises.instructions: Lie on back, knees bent. One hand chest, one on belly. Inhale 4 sec — belly rises, chest stays still. Exhale 6 sec — gentle and complete. Goal: 6 breaths per minute.

breathingExercises.name: Box Breathing 4-4-4-4
breathingExercises.instructions: Inhale through nose 4 sec. Hold comfortably 4 sec. Exhale through mouth 4 sec. Hold empty 4 sec. Repeat 4-6 cycles.
cues: Used by Norwegian athletes for CO₂ tolerance

breathingExercises.name: Thoracic Expansion Breathing
breathingExercises.instructions: Sit tall, hands on sides of ribcage. Inhale slowly and push hands outward — breathe into the SIDES of your chest (lateral costal breathing). Exhale completely.
cues: Breathe into the SIDES — lateral costal breathing
Works against thoracic restriction from kyphosis

breathingExercises.name: 4-7-8 Recovery Breathing
breathingExercises.instructions: Inhale through nose 4 sec. Hold 7 sec. Exhale through mouth 8 sec. Repeat 4 cycles.
cues: 7-sec hold is comfortable — reduce to 5 if initially too long

boltScoreGuide.description: Body Oxygen Level Test — CO₂ tolerance self-assessment (Patrick McKeown, Oxygen Advantage)
steps: Sit comfortably for at least 5 minutes
Take a normal, quiet breath IN through your nose
Take a normal, quiet breath OUT through your nose
Pinch your nose closed with your fingers
Start a timer — count seconds until the FIRST definite urge to breathe (slight tension in throat or abdomen)
Your count = BOLT score (NOT a max breath-hold — stop at first urge)

scale.label: Low
scale.action: Nasal breathing + diaphragmatic work will improve this

scale.label: Good
scale.action: Breathing exercises maintain and develop this
